description_long: This is a traditional axe featuring a robust, single-edged steel blade with a pronounced curve and a wide cutting surface. The blade is securely mounted onto a straight, cylindrical wooden handle, which appears to be made of a medium-brown hardwood. The blade has a broad, bearded design, extending downward to maximize the cutting area, while the poll is flat and unadorned. The handle is smooth, lacking any visible grip enhancements or curvature, and is proportionally long for two-handed use. The mounting of the blade to the handle is classic, with the head fitted over the top of the shaft and likely wedged in place. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the blade or the handle.
description: A traditional axe with a curved steel blade and a straight wooden handle, designed for chopping. The blade is broad and bearded, and the handle is smooth and cylindrical.
description_view_0: The axe is shown from the side, highlighting the full profile of the curved steel blade and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed edge-on, showing the thinness of the blade and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite side profile of the axe, again displaying the broad blade and the smooth wooden handle.
description_view_3: The axe is viewed from the rear, showing the back of the blade and the end of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical wooden handle with a smooth surface and no visible grip features.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, broad, bearded steel blade with a pronounced curve and flat poll.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted over the top of the wooden handle.
